paper_title: Material and methods of Tufa Carbonate Study

methods.description: Process for sampling tufa carbonate from a stream bank for stable isotope analysis.

steps.description: Two parallel vertical cuts about 5 cm apart were made into the carbonate rock using a gasoline-driven cutter, and the material between the cuts was sampled using a hammer and chisel.
steps.searchable_summary: cut and sample carbonate rock using cutter and chisel

steps.description: Six continuous sub-sections within a distance of 5 m were sampled at different accessible places on the large tufa block, and were correlated in the field using distinct parallel-stretching marker layers.
steps.searchable_summary: sample sub-sections of tufa block and correlate using marker layers

steps.description: Five samples of modern tufa carbonate were collected for stable isotope analyses from five different small streams a few metres away from the springs.
steps.searchable_summary: collect modern tufa carbonate samples from streams

steps.description: Five limestone samples were collected from the basement rocks up to 100 m above the tufa-precipitating springs.
steps.searchable_summary: collect limestone samples from basement rocks

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing stable isotopes in carbonate samples using mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Carbonate powders were reacted for stable isotope analysis with 100% phosphoric acid at 75°C using a Kiel III online carbonate preparation line connected to a ThermoFinnigan 252 masspectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: react carbonate powders with phosphoric acid for isotope analysis

steps.description: Reproducibility was checked by replicate analysis of laboratory standards and is better than ±0.06‰ for both ! 13 C and ! 18 O values.
steps.searchable_summary: validate isotope analysis reproducibility with standards

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of thin sections from tufa samples to count couplets and characterize depositional types.

steps.description: Thin sections were prepared from the planar surfaces of the tufa samples that had been used for stable isotope subsampling.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare thin sections from tufa samples

steps.description: The thin sections, which show small holes as a result of the subsampling process, were used to count the white and dark tufa couplets and to characterize the depositional type of the tufa carbonate.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze thin sections to count couplets and characterize depositional type

methods.description: Assessment of the mineralogy of tufa carbonate using X-ray diffraction analysis.

steps.description: The mineralogy of the tufa carbonate was assessed through X-ray diffraction analysis of three samples from 3.0, 2.0 and 1.0 m below the section top using a Philips PW 1050 X-ray spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray diffraction analysis on tufa samples

methods.description: Measurement of U-and Th-series isotopes using multi-static multiple ion counting inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry.

steps.description: U-and Th-series isotope measurements using multi-static multiple ion counting inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (MIC-ICP-MS) were carried out on eight samples.
steps.searchable_summary: measure U-and Th-series isotopes using MIC-ICP-MS

steps.description: A 230 Th/ 232 Th activity ratio of 0.6±0.2 was used for the correction of detrital 230 Th.
steps.searchable_summary: correct detrital 230 Th using activity ratio

methods.method_name: Spectral Analysis of Isotope Time Series
methods.description: Spectral analysis of the ! 18 O and ! 13 C time series using the PAST software package.

steps.description: Spectral analysis of the ! 18 O and ! 13 C time series was carried out using the software package PAST-PAleontological STatistics.
steps.searchable_summary: perform spectral analysis on isotope time series using PAST

steps.description: Artificial stable isotope data were produced for unsampled layers as an average of the stable isotope values from the two neighbouring layers.
steps.searchable_summary: generate artificial isotope data for unsampled layers
